title: Feature request: support SV array query functions like $high and $left
body:
Systemverilog has functions that return information about the bounds of a vector, like `$high/$low` and `$left/$right`. They are convenient helper functions for width-agnostic code/functions.

To give an example, in my use case they are extremely helpful for fixpoint DSP, e.g. automatically calculating the correct vector width and tracking the number of integer/fractional bits:
```verilog
logic [3:-4] a; // a Q4.4 number
logic [3:-8] b; // a Q4.8 number
logic [$high(a)+$high(b)-1 : $low(a)+$low(b)] c = a*b; // automatically generates a 20-bit wide Q8.12 number

logic [$high(c) : 0] c_integer = c[$high(c) : 0];    // extracting the integer part is easy since both bounds can be obtained from a given vector
```

I see no way whatsoever to achieve this with the SV subset supported by Yosys; `read_verilog -sv` fails with `ERROR: Can't resolve function name ``\$left'`

_I use sv2v as a workaround in my flow, although it would be great if yosys would support it natively_
code_blocks:
logic [3:-4] a; // a Q4.4 number
logic [3:-8] b; // a Q4.8 number
logic [$high(a)+$high(b)-1 : $low(a)+$low(b)] c = a*b; // automatically generates a 20-bit wide Q8.12 number

logic [$high(c) : 0] c_integer = c[$high(c) : 0];    // extracting the integer part is easy since both bounds can be obtained from a given vector
